because.text: Accepted in full; step-by-step integer arithmetic (six-step decay with remainders, FALSE En = -32768+256 = -32512) matches the oracle exactly. ADR-004: fourth independent criterion-negative answer; DeepSeek's s1.1 replacement prose (keeps paragraph 1, deletes paragraph 2) taken as textual base for adoption, merged with Codex's MUST-NOT clauses; no new vector needed, EV-LIT-FORCE already pins the behavior. ADR-005: R1 - criterion answer 'none exists today' addresses Gemini's R2 use case head-on (wave layer is navigation-scoped; phase pin stays visible; future protocols can add full pins without breaking vectors). MAINTAINER ADJUDICATION OF THE SPLIT: R1 wins 2:1 - both R1 answers defeat the boolean-app-invisibility scenario within R1 (phase visibility + opt-in full pins), R2 invents values nobody computed (DeepSeek's Trinity-vs-MAX asymmetry point), maintainer position final: R1. Normative FALSE row {49152,0,-32512} verified and adopted; WV-FALSE-DERIVED/WV-ITER-DECAY merged with Codex's vector set; pin-table layer scheduled.

subject.note: DeepSeek v4 Pro ADR-004/005 gate review: Option 2 (4th criterion-negative answer); R1 with normative FALSE row - ADR-005 decision candidate R1 at 2:1
